title: paysonia auriculata
intro: Paysonia auriculata, also known as Eastern Buttercup or Golden Ragwort, is a flowering plant native to the eastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its root, which is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Paysonia auriculata include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to soothe respiratory issues. Paysonia auriculata is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can also be used in salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
